question.content: Compare the target ratio using the threshold line and the peak positions. Answer format: exactly one of >, <, or =; do not include words, units, or any other symbols.

step_1.content: Use the threshold lines and waveform peaks in the original graph to compare the numerator Vp-Ve while keeping the denominator Ve-Vm fixed.
step_1.key_point: The index eta increases when the peak exceeds electrical threshold by a larger margin.

step_3.content: In cycles with only slow waves, the peak is around or below electrical threshold, so Vp-Ve is small or non-positive.
step_3.key_point: eta is smaller in the no-spike cycle.

step_5.content: In cycles with spike bursts and clear tension rise, the membrane peak exceeds electrical threshold by more, increasing the numerator.
step_5.key_point: eta is larger in the spike-associated cycle.
